description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0 Docker image with two stages: build and deploy. In the build stage add a build job that caches the .nuget directory keyed by $CI_PROJECT_ID, runs dotnet tool restore, dotnet fantomas --check src tests, dotnet build --packages .nuget/packages, apt-get update && apt-get install libxml2, dotnet test --no-build --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --logger:junit, a sed command to extract line coverage, dotnet reportgenerator -reports:\"tests/Jita.Tests/TestResults/*/coverage.cobertura.xml\" -targetdir:public/coverage, dotnet fsdocs build --strict --output public/docs, and dotnet pack --no-restore -c Release -o publish, and always publishes the publish/, public/, and tests/Jita.Tests/TestResults/ directories as artifacts together with a Cobertura coverage report and a JUnit test report. Add a nuget job in the deploy stage that depends on the build job, uses the same .nuget cache, runs only on the master branch, and executes echo publish/*nupkg | xargs -n1 dotnet nuget push --api-key $NUGET_API_KEY --source https://nuget.org to push the generated .nupkg files to NuGet.org. Add a pages job also in the deploy stage, dependent on build, runs only on master, runs a trivial true script, and publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact. Ensure the pipeline captures line‑coverage using the regex /Line Coverage ([0-9.]+)%/ and stores the coverage report at tests/Jita.Tests/TestResults/*/coverage.cobertura.xml.
